Zen Technologies Limited ( (IN:ZENTEC) ) has shared an update.

Zen Technologies Limited has transferred 1,000 equity shares to its employees under the Zen Technologies Limited Employee Stock Option Plan-2021. This transfer, executed from the company’s Employees Welfare Trust, does not alter the company’s paid-up share capital, and the newly allotted shares are identical to existing ones, ensuring no change in the company’s equity structure.

More about Zen Technologies Limited

Zen Technologies Limited operates in the technology industry, focusing on providing innovative solutions, including simulation technology and training systems. The company is listed on the BSE Limited and the National Stock Exchange of India Limited.

Castellum AB ( (SE:CAST) ) has provided an update.

Castellum AB has announced a consent solicitation to amend the terms of its outstanding EMTN bonds. The proposed changes aim to align with its ‘Back to Basics’ strategy, focusing on core operations and maintaining an investment-grade rating. This move is expected to enhance operational efficiency and refocus its property portfolio.

Neometals Ltd ( (AU:NMT) ) has shared an announcement.

Neometals Ltd announced the results of its Annual General Meeting, highlighting that all resolutions were carried with significant majority votes. The meeting’s outcomes, including the approval of performance rights and re-election of directors, reflect strong shareholder support and are expected to positively impact the company’s strategic initiatives and stakeholder confidence.

More about Neometals Ltd

Neometals Ltd is a company focused on delivering stakeholder value through sustainable production of critical and valuable materials essential for a cleaner future. The company is involved in commercializing low-cost sustainable processing solutions for critical materials and exploring and developing mining operations at its Barrambie Gold Project. Neometals’ portfolio includes lithium chemicals, vanadium recovery, and mineral assets like Barrambie Gold and Barrambie Titanium and Vanadium.

Tian Teck Land Limited ( (HK:0266) ) has shared an update.

Tian Teck Land Limited reported its unaudited consolidated results for the six months ending September 30, 2025, showing a decrease in revenue and a significant loss due to valuation losses on investment properties. Despite a gross profit of HK$102.5 million, the company faced a net loss of HK$149.6 million, impacting both equity shareholders and non-controlling interests. The company’s financial position shows a decrease in net assets, reflecting challenges in the real estate market.

data.title: Triton Minerals Completes Unmarketable Parcel Share Sale Facility
data.description:
TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Triton Minerals Limited ( (AU:TON) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Triton Minerals Limited has completed a share sale facility for holders of unmarketable parcels of shares, which are defined as holdings valued at less than A$500. This initiative allowed shareholders with small holdings to sell their shares cost-effectively, reducing administrative costs for the company. The facility resulted in the sale of approximately 23.6 million shares, representing 1.5% of the company’s issued capital and affecting 54% of the shareholders on the register. This move is expected to streamline operations and improve efficiency in managing shareholder records.

More about Triton Minerals Limited

Triton Minerals Limited is a company operating in the mining industry, primarily focused on the exploration and development of graphite projects. The company is listed on the Australian Securities Exchange (ASX) under the ticker ‘TON’.

Mandom ( (JP:4917) ) just unveiled an update.

Mandom Corporation announced a response to an amendment statement filed by Kalon Holdings Co., Ltd. regarding a tender offer for Mandom’s common shares. The amendment includes changes to the purchase terms, notably adjusting the share price to 2,520 yen. Mandom’s board will evaluate these changes to determine their impact on corporate value and shareholder interests, with a decision on their stance forthcoming.
